exemplar.title: What is the relation between personal experiences and knowledge?

detailedExemplarData.title: What is the relation between personal experiences and knowledge?

strengths: Clear identification of three specific, real-world objects with contextualized images and captions
Well-framed guiding question and exhibition title that establish a coherent TOK focus
Justification for each objectâs inclusion demonstrates understanding of personal experience in knowledge acquisition
weaknesses: Explanations rely heavily on general description and anecdote with limited use of explicit TOK terminology
Repetition of key ideas (e.g., tacit knowledge, lived experience) without deeper theoretical development
Links between objects and the IA prompt lack precision, sustained analysis and external evidence beyond personal examples
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exhibition meets the descriptors for the 5â6 band by clearly naming three objects, situating them in real-world contexts, and offering justifications linked to personal experience and knowledge. The studentâs structure is coherent, with a strong guiding question and relevant imagery. However, the commentary remains largely descriptive and repetitive, with minimal integration of formal TOK concepts or external evidence. The links to the IA prompt are understandable but lack the depth and precision required for the 7â8 band. Given these strengths and weaknesses, and following the examinerâs final mark of 5, this score accurately reflects a solid but unexceptional performance at the lower end of the 5â6 band.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student clearly names three specific, real-world objects (personal soccer ball, individual passport, personal cellphone) and states their contexts. For each object, the student does link the object to the chosen IA prompt about personal experience and knowledge, and attempts to justify why it belongs in the exhibition. However, the explanations rely heavily on general description and repetition (e.g., âlived experience,â âfirst-hand knowledgeâ) with limited use of explicit TOK concepts or critical comparisons. Evidence is largely anecdotal, and there are no references beyond the studentâs own experience. While the links are understandable, they lack depth, precision, and sustained analysis expected at the higher bands. Hence the work fits best in the 5â6 band, but toward its lower end because justification is present but not consistently strong or well-supported.

annotations.comment: The exhibitionâs title is clear and immediately establishes the focus on TOK exploration.
The guiding question is prominently displayed, providing a clear TOK focus for the exhibition.
Include the exact wording of the IA prompt in the introduction to make the connection to the assessment criteria explicit.
The student frames the exploration by defining personal experienceâs role in knowledge acquisition, providing coherent context.
Consider referencing specific TOK terminology (e.g., âways of knowingâ or âknowledge frameworkâ) to ground your thesis in TOK theory early.
Object 1 is clearly identified with a descriptive title, which helps the reader follow the structure of the exhibition.
The photo of the soccer ball is original and contextualized with an appropriate caption and author attribution.
The justification of the soccer ball example remains largely anecdotal; integrate formal TOK concepts to deepen the analysis.
Strengthen the link between the soccer ball example and the IA prompt by explicitly naming the promptâs key terms in your commentary.
Repetition of âtacit knowledgeâ is evident; to advance your argument, explore its theoretical basis and link to TOK literature.
The passport is introduced clearly with its context and significance effectively outlined.
The photograph and caption evoke personal travel experiences, grounding your claims in concrete evidence.
In your discussion of cultural exposure, incorporate TOK concepts such as âshared knowledgeâ vs âpersonal knowledgeâ to sharpen your analysis.
The text conflates âexperienceâ and âknowledgeâ broadly; more precise TOK distinctions (e.g., propositional vs procedural) would clarify your claims.
You might contrast experiential knowledge with propositional knowledge directly to deepen the justification of your passport example.
This statement restates earlier points on experiential vs theoretical knowledge without advancing the TOK argument.
Reference specific ways of knowing (e.g., perception, language) when discussing how passport experiences shape personal knowledge.
Object 3 is clearly presented with relevant context tying it to the chosen optional theme.
The mobile phone image and caption effectively illustrate the objectâs role in knowledge acquisition.
Consider explicitly linking this technological object back to the exhibition prompt in the caption to reinforce its relevance.
The description of the phoneâs functions remains descriptive; it needs deeper analysis of how technology frames and mediates knowledge.
Integrate elements of the TOK knowledge framework to analyse how the mobile phone shapes ways of knowing and areas of knowledge.
The discussion of active vs passive knowledge demonstrates a nuanced understanding of how experience shapes knowing.
The conclusion summarizes each objectâs role well, but synthesizing insights across all three would form a more cohesive argument.
To elevate your conclusion, draw out broader implications for TOK questions about the nature and construction of knowledge.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
